116995.explanation: It is about quality, not gender.

116996.explanation: The interests of the residents always come first. Decisions must be practical, honest, transparent, and applicable, ensuring the voice of the IJsselstein resident is heard.

117002.explanation: The LDIJ opposes an asylum seeker center in IJsselstein. Although IJsselstein has obligations under the dispersion law and does not shy away from its responsibility, the LDIJ wants to collaborate with surrounding municipalities for joint reception efforts, where safety is a key theme.

117003.explanation: This is not an issue in IJsselstein.

117007.explanation: We do not consider a fireworks display as a core task of the municipality.

117009.explanation: Entrepreneurs must simply comply with the law and are free to conduct business within those boundaries.

117012.explanation: The local broadcaster will receive the funds legally available for this purpose. Given its limited reach, we will not allocate extra funds.

117018.explanation: Deploying camera surveillance at locations with increased disturbances is important to us. This must also be followed up when illegal activities are detected.

117019.explanation: We already have sufficient capacity with the BOKA.

117024.explanation: The LDIJ advocates a strict approach to involve everyone in society. We want to prevent a group of IJsselsteiners from becoming isolated. We do not write off people on social assistance but want to encourage them to develop their talents and learn Dutch.

117026.explanation: The municipality should encourage people to earn their own income to escape poverty. Among other things, by requiring (mandatory) Dutch language learning and registration at employment agencies, we want to stimulate people to rise out of poverty.

117029.explanation: On residential streets where children play, 30 km/h is an appropriate speed. On some main roads, 50 km/h can still be permitted.

117030.explanation: Care is important and must be of sufficient quality for everyone. There should be no division based on religion, culture, or belief.

117033.explanation: With the completion of De Kroon, the capacity is sufficient for the time being.

117034.explanation: This is not a matter for the municipality but for the retailers.

117035.explanation: Our IJsselstein residents have also been waiting for a very long time.

117038.explanation: A new community center in IJsselveld East is important for neighborhood cohesion. We want a community center in every neighborhood.

117040.explanation: We see no room for a coffee shop.

117041.explanation: We adhere to the housing vision: 30 percent social, 40 percent middle segment, and 30 percent expensive. Additionally, we want to make starter homes accessible through the KoopStart scheme, where buyers receive a 20 percent discount on the purchase price.

117042.explanation: IJsselstein must retain its character. However, limited height increases are allowed if they fit the surroundings.

117043.explanation: The LDIJ wants neighborhood walk-in centers that organize low-threshold activities such as coffee mornings, creative and play activities; courses for seniors (fall prevention, digital resilience); library activities like borrowing books, language skills, and homework support.

117044.explanation: Free parking in garages and the free first residents' permit must be maintained.

117046.explanation: The Windotter may remain; no additional windmills will be installed.

117047.explanation: Depending on the outcome of the pilot. However, as far as the LDIJ is concerned, cycling should be allowed during the times when deliveries are permitted.

116995.explanation: We value diversity and representation. IJsselstein residents should see themselves reflected in the city’s executive. Equal gender distribution contributes to this. Moreover, a mixed team generally performs better.

116996.explanation: We do not make decisions about IJsselsteiners but together with them. It is important to increase participation through, for example, citizens’ assemblies, referenda, and good participation with clear feedback on why input is or isn’t incorporated.

117002.explanation: Every municipality must make a fair contribution to the reception of asylum seekers. We must meet our quotas, possibly in cooperation with nearby municipalities. We consider humane and small-scale reception important.

117003.explanation: We consider healthy and humane housing important, on private property or elsewhere. Employers are held responsible for housing according to the SNF label: a quality mark for the housing of labor migrants.

117007.explanation: Now that a national fireworks ban is coming, it is important for the municipality to organize a festive New Year’s Eve. A fireworks or laser show is among the options.

117009.explanation: Agreed, although we favor co-financing: entrepreneurs also benefit from making their buildings more accessible. We also find it important that everyone in society can participate, including people with disabilities. We are happy to invest in that.

117012.explanation: The local broadcaster forms an important pillar supporting local democracy. Without independent journalism, democracy is under pressure. To increase trust in politics, it is important to clearly explain decisions made in the council via the local broadcaster.

117018.explanation: We are not opposed to this, but it is not the starting point. It begins with prevention and good information to make IJsselstein safer. Additionally, deploying neighborhood fathers and neighborhood coaches is necessary. If that does not help, we opt for more camera surveillance.

117019.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must have a safe place to go. The municipality of IJsselstein already collaborates with Veilig Thuis, which receives reports of domestic violence, investigates, and provides advice. Unfortunately, domestic violence and femicide still occur in IJsselstein, including last year.

117024.explanation: People receiving social assistance are already actively looking for a job, but we believe it is important they also remain active and participate in society. Obligatory social contributions should not replace regular paid work. We also base this on trust.

117026.explanation: We want an income up to 130 percent of the social assistance standard as an indication. There should also be more room for customization, and applications for income assistance should be simplified. In cases of doubt, the applicant receives the benefit of the doubt: trust is the foundation.

117029.explanation: Lowering the maximum speed within built-up areas increases road safety, can improve traffic flow on main roads, and is good for the city’s air quality. Moreover, better traffic flow often leads to shorter travel times after lowering the maximum speed.

117030.explanation: Agreed, this also aligns with Article 1 of the Constitution. It is important, however, that the care provided is based on scientific research and can be qualified as medical care. The care provider must not make decisions or judgments about ethical issues.

117033.explanation: With the arrival of the business park De Kroon, IJsselstein will have enough business parks in the coming years. Where possible, commercial buildings on sites adjacent to residential neighborhoods can be transformed into homes or small residential areas, in consultation with the owners.

117034.explanation: This promotes the economic vitality of the city and the vibrant character of IJsselstein. This must be organized in consultation with the city center entrepreneurs' associations. They must also be able to manage it.

117035.explanation: If you don't do that, significantly more shelter locations will be needed. We have limited space for that in IJsselstein. We want more social rental housing (35 percent in new construction) alongside 40 percent affordable owner-occupied homes. We also want to give priority to IJsselstein residents.

117038.explanation: Studio 10 will no longer receive funding, but will be demolished and likely replaced by a new community center with housing above. This will also create more homes. We consider this a positive development and it costs money.

117040.explanation: There is not enough demand and it could increase insecurity in the area. Everyone is free to make their own choices, but we do not want to encourage drug use.

117041.explanation: We want even 35 percent social rental housing because waiting lists in IJsselstein are too long. Everyone must have a roof over their head. Affordable living is possible with 35 percent social rental and 40 percent mid-range owner-occupied homes.

117042.explanation: We do not want to build too high. And we want more homes in IJsselstein, because only 200 houses have been built in the past five years! That must change, but it can only happen if apartments are also built. We believe higher than the current buildings does not suit IJsselstein.

117043.explanation: Connection with the neighborhood is important for the atmosphere in IJsselstein. We want people to be able to meet each other in a neighborhood center, organize fun activities together, and build and expand networks through a neighborhood center.

117044.explanation: We want to make the city center low-traffic by car, while preserving space for loading, unloading, and parking for residents of the inner city. Visitors can park on the outskirts or come to the center by tram, bus, bicycle, or on foot.

117046.explanation: We have always advocated for a combination of wind and solar energy. Research into wind energy showed there is hardly any space for wind turbines in IJsselstein. Therefore, we want to collaborate regionally on wind energy for more affordable and sustainable energy.

117047.explanation: The pilot is still ongoing, but we hear positive feedback from residents and shopkeepers about the bicycle ban in the city center. Cyclists cannot ride through the shopping street but can go around the center. More (secured) bike parking facilities are needed.

116995.explanation: The IJsselstein VVD finds it important that more women participate in politics. At the same time, we believe leaders should primarily be selected based on competencies, experience, and support.

116996.explanation: The IJsselstein VVD believes elected representatives have the task of making complex decisions on behalf of residents.

117002.explanation: This is a task assigned to us by the national government, and IJsselstein has already proven it can contribute. Of course, we critically assess what is feasible, manageable, and safe. The arrival of an asylum seekers center must not come at the expense of housing locations.

117003.explanation: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should be able to decide for themselves whether and how to arrange housing for seasonal workers. It must be legal, safe, and meet the required standards.

117007.explanation: The municipality can facilitate this, but initiative should come from residents and entrepreneurs.

117009.explanation: The IJsselstein VVD supports measures that improve accessibility for people with disabilities. Entrepreneurial responsibility remains important, and the municipality takes a facilitating role.

117012.explanation: The local broadcaster plays an important role in IJsselstein. The municipality complies with its contribution under the Media Act, which should be sufficient.

117018.explanation: The IJsselstein VVD aims to expand (mobile) camera surveillance. Since 2020, VVD has been monitoring live camera footage. This nips unsafe situations in the bud. We will continue this without compromise.

117019.explanation: The IJsselstein VVD believes victims of violence must be protected and their safety ensured.

117024.explanation: The IJsselstein VVD thinks welfare recipients should be able to perform socially valuable (volunteer) work to promote participation, personal responsibility, and chances of regular employment.

117026.explanation: The VVD believes support for poverty should primarily go to those who need it most.

117029.explanation: Many roads in IJsselstein that improve traffic safety are already 30 km/h zones. This is also almost everywhere the case in residential areas and 15 km/h in home zones. If there are specific roads or locations where 30 km/h is needed for safety, we support it.

117030.explanation: The municipality provides basic care. Residents can organize additional wishes themselves and will be reimbursed for them.

117033.explanation: Businesses are good for the local economy and employment. Wherever possible, we expand business parks.

117034.explanation: We give entrepreneurs space. We support retailers' associations to make joint agreements. Among other things, the city thrives on shopping Sundays, thanks to the entrepreneurs!

117035.explanation: We also do this for residents working in education, healthcare, the police, or as volunteers with our fire department.

117038.explanation: Due to damage caused to Studio 10, the building is no longer safe. The cost to repair it is too high, making demolition logical.

117040.explanation: The IJsselstein VVD supports individual freedom of choice but it must be strictly regulated to limit nuisance and drug-related crime. The municipality must always ensure coffeshops comply with the law and that minors are denied access.

117041.explanation: There is also a great need for affordable (owner-occupied) homes. Building affordable (owner-occupied) homes also creates opportunities for social rental residents to move up to a house, freeing social rental homes.

117042.explanation: The housing shortage is severe. We want to help IJsselstein residents find a home. On the city’s outskirts, apartment complexes with affordable homes higher than three floors can be built.

117043.explanation: The desire for a community center should come from the neighborhood. However, we must invest in our shopping centers. These are our neighborhood centers.

117044.explanation: We maintain the first two hours of free parking in the Overtoom and Eiteren parking garages.

117046.explanation: The VVD was, is, and remains opposed to the construction of wind turbines in IJsselstein.

117047.explanation: Open shops, pedestrians walking: this makes shopping in our town more pleasant and safer for everyone.

116995.explanation: The parties forming the coalition will ensure someone with good qualities takes the alderman positions. This can be a man or a woman. It is desirable for both women and men to be in the college.

116996.explanation: The CDA is critical of referenda. Complex issues require careful consideration and nuance, not a yes/no choice. We choose a strong municipal council and good prior participation so residents can think along early and decisions are broadly supported and responsibly made.

117002.explanation: The CDA stands for solidarity and carefulness. Refugee shelter is important but only if a suitable location exists that fits the environment and has support. If not, we prefer quality over haste and seek better solutions with partners.

117003.explanation: The CDA believes housing for seasonal workers must be safe, humane, and well-integrated into the environment. On-farm housing often leads to excessive pressure, insufficient supervision, and mixing of work and living. Municipal coordination and suitable locations are necessary.

117007.explanation: The CDA supports a central fireworks show on New Year’s Eve. This ensures safety, less nuisance, and less harm to people, animals, and the environment. Celebrating together strengthens city cohesion and offers a festive alternative to consumer fireworks.

117009.explanation: The CDA believes everyone, including people with disabilities, must be able to participate in society. Municipality and entrepreneurs can help each other make buildings accessible and promote inclusion.

117012.explanation: The CDA thinks the local broadcaster has an important role but must operate within the existing subsidy. Additional funding is not responsible. Municipal resources are scarce and should prioritize core tasks such as care, safety, and livability.

117018.explanation: The CDA does not find extra camera surveillance desirable. Cameras can help in specific locations, but safety primarily requires visible people in the neighborhood, not additional cameras. Camera surveillance affects privacy, is costly, and limits nuisance prevention. Prevention and enforcement officers work better.

117019.explanation: The CDA believes IJsselstein should have a women's shelter. Victims of domestic violence deserve immediate, safe shelter close to home. Local availability shortens waiting times, increases child protection, and promotes recovery and good cooperation with care partners.

117024.explanation: The CDA holds that people on social assistance benefits should do socially meaningful work. This increases participation, routine, and self-worth, prevents isolation, and maintains skills. The municipality guides and supports so that work contributes to opportunities for transition to paid employment.

117026.explanation: The CDA believes IJsselstein should not raise the income threshold for poverty assistance. Aid is intended for those who truly need it. Broadening it makes policy less targeted, increases costs, and reduces support. This way, support remains affordable, fair, and available.

117029.explanation: The CDA considers 30 km/h important in residential neighborhoods where possible, but there must also remain roads with 50 km/h to maintain traffic flow and accessibility. Safety and livability should go hand in hand.

117030.explanation: The CDA believes that freedom of choice in healthcare is important but recognizes the healthcare market is under great pressure. Due to staff shortages and scarcity, matching based on religion or culture is not always feasible. Good, accessible care for everyone must come first.

117033.explanation: The CDA believes IJsselstein must handle scarce space carefully. With the recent extension of nine hectares at the De Kroon industrial area, the demand has been met. Further expansion would come at the expense of housing, greenery, and livability.

117034.explanation: The CDA observes that twelve shopping Sundays per year are sufficient in practice. Signals from entrepreneurs show that they themselves prefer fewer shopping Sundays because too few shops open on current shopping Sundays.

117035.explanation: The CDA follows national regulations. Currently, status holders assigned to the municipality of IJsselstein are given priority for social rental housing. The municipality adheres to this policy.

117038.explanation: According to the CDA, Studio 10 is indispensable. Youth and elderly use the community center, for example at the Cruyff Court or cooking courses. It must remain in the neighborhood and receive support from the municipality.

117040.explanation: According to the CDA, IJsselstein should not allow a coffee shop. We choose a healthy, safe environment for young people and adults and want to prevent problematic drug use as much as possible.

117041.explanation: The CDA believes that a significant portion of new construction must be affordable. At least 30 percent social rental ensures that starters, middle incomes, and vulnerable groups can find a place in IJsselstein, matching local needs and livability.

117042.explanation: The CDA recognizes there is a great need for housing. The municipality has no uniform standard height for all homes. The height of the houses depends on the project and location in IJsselstein. The CDA favors an appropriate height that does not detract from the surroundings.

117043.explanation: The CDA believes every neighborhood deserves its own community center. This creates a meeting place for young and old, strengthens social cohesion, and promotes involvement in the neighborhood.

117044.explanation: The CDA wants an attractive and accessible city center. Parking prices must remain balanced with livability, accessibility, and supporting local entrepreneurs.

117046.explanation: The CDA does not opt for large wind turbines in IJsselstein. Earlier studies showed that installation according to current rules is not possible, partly due to health risks for residents. We prefer to focus on other sustainable solutions that suit the city.

117047.explanation: The CDA is neither fundamentally for nor against cycling in the city center, but a good balance must remain between accessibility and experience. The bicycle trial will be evaluated to find the best solution.

116995.explanation: We strive for more equality. After a female mayor, we are ready for even more and better representation of women in politics.

116996.explanation: We must exclude nothing to ask residents' opinions. Not every topic is suited for a yes/no referendum question. Ultimately, the municipal council makes the decision.

117002.explanation: The municipality must fulfill its legal obligation and commit to accommodating asylum seekers. All municipalities must contribute their part. We prefer to do this together with neighboring municipalities.

117003.explanation: Good housing can also be organized outside the company's own industrial site.

117007.explanation: There is a fireworks ban. It is not up to the municipality to organize this. However, the municipality should facilitate a local New Year's Eve celebration organized by entrepreneurs.

117009.explanation: The municipality must better communicate this requirement and also set a good example in its own buildings. It can help entrepreneurs along the way.

117012.explanation: Local media is essential and of great importance.

117018.explanation: Before installing more cameras, we want a plan to deploy them efficiently and effectively. Cameras impact residents' privacy.

117019.explanation: If you cannot stay at home, it is important there is a place where you can go. We must facilitate that for IJsselstein residents and in IJsselstein as well.

117024.explanation: People on benefits should work as much as possible. Volunteer work is also work. If for any reason you cannot contribute temporarily, your right to benefits does not lapse.

117026.explanation: We want as many people as possible to participate. People's security of existence leads us, not a fixed percentage.

117029.explanation: For livability and safety, 30 km/h should be the default in all neighborhoods.

117030.explanation: The healthcare system is under pressure. The principle should be that everyone can always choose a caregiver who suits them. If that is not available, providing care takes precedence over the ideal caregiver.

117033.explanation: Currently, a site is being developed along the A2 highway.

117034.explanation: Entrepreneurs should decide as much as possible about their opening hours. If there is a need for extended hours alongside many activities and elements, we will facilitate that.

117035.explanation: The asylum chain is under pressure; IJsselstein is contributing to the solution. This concerns a small portion of the freed-up homes per year in our city.

117038.explanation: The community center has been demolished. The municipality must build a new community center as soon as possible and organize activities in the neighborhood in the meantime, together with youth work and the housing association.

117040.explanation: The legalization of soft drugs remains absent; the tolerance policy leads to an undesirable mixing of the underworld and upper world. We do not exclude such an enterprise outright. This can only happen in consultation with the environment, at a location where it is feasible, and under the condition of proper supervision.

117041.explanation: This will become a legal requirement. Housing is a fundamental right and there are currently too few affordable homes.

117042.explanation: We need more houses but also want to preserve green space. It remains odd that all tall residential flats are now in one neighborhood. This should be more balanced. Each new construction project should consider what fits best where.

117043.explanation: Ensure more togetherness; bring the municipality, library, and other facilities to the neighborhood. The municipality must be there for the people and with the people.

117044.explanation: Space in the center is limited and costly. Car users pay for the space they use. 'Free' parking costs the IJsselstein taxpayer a lot of money; that is unfair policy. We will create more bike parking spots and it should remain free.

117046.explanation: We do not consider covering the beautiful polder landscape with solar panels an option; one wind turbine already prevents that.

117047.explanation: Buying cheese on Monday morning is perfectly fine by bike. Cycling during busy times is not advisable. A cycling ban does not help people leave the car at home.

116995.explanation: CU IJsselstein favors a mixed team, where each coalition party can provide their best candidate. Therefore, a fixed ratio cannot be enforced.

116996.explanation: CU IJsselstein actively involves residents in political decision-making through consultation and participation as part of various policy topics. A referendum does not do justice to the complexity of many issues and the trade-offs that must be made.

117002.explanation: CU IJsselstein supports small-scale refugee shelter. This way, the municipality retains control. However, we believe this must not come at the expense of housing for other target groups, so any asylum seekers' center should be built outside the existing built-up area.

117003.explanation: CU IJsselstein finds it important that seasonal workers receive good, fair housing at normal rents. Employers must arrange this, preferably in temporary housing built outside the existing built-up area.

117007.explanation: CU IJsselstein supports a fireworks or laser show because it motivates residents to celebrate together without the nuisance of fireworks.

117009.explanation: CU IJsselstein supports having shops accessible and public toilets in the city center. Currently, we have no indications that adjustments are needed, but we are willing to contribute to solutions, although we primarily believe entrepreneurs are responsible.

117012.explanation: CU IJsselstein supports a strong democracy. Besides dualism, good journalism is also desirable. We support Omroep Lekstroom with a subsidy and are willing to contribute extra if necessary.

117018.explanation: Safety in a medium-sized city like IJsselstein is not improved by extensive camera surveillance, although in some places it is unavoidable. We prefer to focus on social cohesion in neighborhoods and strong involvement via associations to promote safety.

117019.explanation: We already have a BOKA location. This is important when people experience relationship problems. Additionally, we must ensure people work on their relationships in time and have access to support to prevent domestic violence.

117024.explanation: CU IJsselstein has long supported activating people on welfare through suitable work, which may also include volunteer work. This was always difficult in our council, but now opinions are ready to really implement this. Not mandatory, but based on motivation.

117026.explanation: IJsselstein currently has a relatively high threshold for special social assistance (namely 110 percent of the welfare standard). The threshold for the collective health insurance and the U-pass is slightly higher (125 percent). These values have been shown to guarantee basic livelihood security.

117029.explanation: CU IJsselstein wants to improve safety on main roads by building bicycle paths separated from cars. Within residential neighborhoods, we want a maximum speed of 30 km/h, but this is already mostly the case.

117030.explanation: It is important that people trust and understand the caregivers who provide care or assistance for an extended period. It helps if people have a choice themselves. For incidental or emergency care, this is less relevant.

117033.explanation: In IJsselstein we have chosen to develop the business park De Kroon. The ChristenUnie wants to then address existing old business parks and use them more efficiently, because there is basically no space left for new business parks.

117034.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports a rhythm in which society also takes rest, such as on Sundays. IJsselstein currently has a limited number of shopping Sundays. This is a mixed success for entrepreneurs. The CU does not want to change the current policy.

117035.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes municipalities are obliged to enforce the law; otherwise, they cannot require residents to comply with it. At the same time, the municipality must consider alternatives because Ukrainians will need housing from March 2027. CU IJsselstein wants temporary housing.

117038.explanation: CU IJsselstein wants community centers in every neighborhood. We think these can be combined in existing buildings and organizations, but we want to invest in this.

117040.explanation: The ChristenUnie is not in favor of legalizing soft drugs. Not only do lives get destroyed by drugs, but there is also a lot of crime involved. We support a drug-free society.

117041.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports 30 percent social rental/home ownership. More important is that we finally build solidly, delivering more than 100 homes per year. We should also make it easier for multiple people to live in the same house through forms of co-living/splitting.

117042.explanation: The ChristenUnie is not in favor of high residential towers in IJsselstein. The maximum building height is limited by our historic city center and the windmill. Moreover, high-rise buildings do not necessarily contribute to a livable city.

117043.explanation: The ChristenUnie strengthens neighborhoods with meeting places in every neighborhood. This builds strong communities and prevents loneliness.

117044.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds it unacceptable that paid parking from 2028 will cost more than five euros per hour. This is unnecessary and undesirable. Our proposal is to keep only the first hour free in parking garages so that street parking increases less sharply.

117046.explanation: The ChristenUnie values sustainable energy. Now that it is clear there is no suitable location for large-scale wind energy in IJsselstein, we must continue focusing on solar energy. At the same time, surrounding municipalities could possibly supply energy when the sun is not shining via wind energy.

117047.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds the accessibility of the city center for cyclists important. Sufficient places to park bicycles are important. We also support a bike-free zone but want to carefully listen to the evaluation by residents and adjust and improve plans accordingly.

statements.theme: Bicycle ban in the city centre
statements.title: The municipality should lift the bicycle ban in the city center.
More business parks/industrial estates should be built in IJsselstein.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The residents of IJsselstein should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van IJsselstein moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: More camera surveillance should be installed in IJsselstein.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: IJsselstein should stop funding the community center Studio 10 on Journaalpad.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: More high residential apartment buildings should be allowed in IJsselstein.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: IJsselstein should have a women's shelter (domestic violence refuge).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: Of the newly built homes in IJsselstein, at least 30% should be social-rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social-rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in IJsselstein moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: There should be wind turbines in IJsselstein.

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.
